title: Open Problem of Esperet, Labourel, and Ochem (induced universal graphs for odd degree)
statement_text: Close the gap between the lower bound $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta)) \geq c\, n^{\Delta/2}$ and the upper bound $O(n^{\Delta/2+1/2-1/\Delta})$ for $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta))$ when $\Delta$ is odd.
context_text: Butler showed $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta)) = \Theta(n^{\Delta/2})$ for even $\Delta$ but only $O(n^{\Delta/2+1/2})$ for odd $\Delta$. Esperet, Labourel, and Ochem improved the odd-$\Delta$ upper bound to $O(n^{\Delta/2+1/2-1/\Delta})$ using a reduction of Chung and sparse universal graphs, and explicitly mentioned the problem of closing the remaining gap to the $\Omega(n^{\Delta/2})$ lower bound.
paper_contribution: Proves that $g_v(\mathcal{H}(n,\Delta)) = O(n^{\Delta/2})$ for every integer $\Delta \geq 2$, matching the lower bound up to a constant factor and fully resolving the problem.
notes: No formal labeled theorem environment in the source; the problem is stated in prose. PDF source — math notation appears largely intact.
paper_title: Optimal induced universal graphs for bounded-degree graphs
